facts.title: FiO2 percent-vs-fraction inconsistency
facts.claim: DadosProntuario.FiO2Validator constrains FiO2 to 0 or 21-100 (a percentage), yet the P/F ratio and every threshold consuming it (SOFA respiratory 400/300/200/100; ventilation weaning 150-300; ARDS/Berlin) assume FiO2 as a fraction 0.21-1.0. Some sites divide FiO2 by 100, some compare it as a fraction directly; unit tests set fio2=1 attribute-directly, bypassing the validator and masking the defect. With validator-conformant (percentage) data the ratio is ~100x too small, driving the SOFA respiratory sub-score to max and spuriously classifying severe ARDS; fraction-only comparisons (e.g. fio2 < 0.4) never fire on percentage data. Affected: RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-002, RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-008, RULE-VENTILACAO-004, RULE-VENTILACAO-005, RULE-VENTILACAO-006, RULE-VENTILACAO-011, RULE-SEDACAO-004, RULE-SEDACAO-016, RULE-SEPSE-020, RULE-EVOLUCOES-001, RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-017.

facts.title: Vasopressor dose-unit chaos (ml / ml/h vs mcg/kg/min vs mcg/kg/h)
facts.claim: Noradrenaline/inotrope thresholds mix a raw infusion VOLUME (Noradrenalina.quantidade in ml) or an infusion RATE in ml/h with the clinical-standard/facade-displayed weight-based rate mcg/kg/min; ml/h -> mcg/kg/min is NOT convertible without drug concentration and patient weight. Separately, the same >0.5 noradrenaline cutoff is labelled mcg/kg/min in RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016 but mcg/kg/h in RULE-ESTABILIDADE-024 - a 60x discrepancy on the same number. Infusion-volume-vs-rate affected: RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-005, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-007, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-008, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-009, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-019, RULE-SEDACAO-005, RULE-SEDACAO-010, RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-029, RULE-SEPSE-014. Per-minute-vs-per-hour label drift affected: RULE-ESTABILIDADE-007, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-008, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-019, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-024, RULE-SEDACAO-010, RULE-SINAIS-VITAIS-029, RULE-TRILHAS-ENGINE-017.

facts.title: Lactate units mg/dL vs mmol/L
facts.claim: Lactate thresholds are used without a consistent unit convention; sepsis/shock pathways and their facades disagree on whether a lactate value is mg/dL or mmol/L (roughly a 9x conversion factor), so the same numeric cutoff denotes very different severities depending on the ingested unit. Affected: RULE-EFICIENCIA-008, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-005, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-020, RULE-SEPSE-013, RULE-SEPSE-050, RULE-SEPSE-058, RULE-SEPSE-059, RULE-SEPSE-061, RULE-VENTILACAO-012.

facts.title: Facade-text vs model-predicate threshold drift
facts.claim: The clinician-facing recommendation/alert text (facade layer: get_detalhe, get_payload_*, payload_*_v3) states a threshold, unit, or criterion number that the actual v3 model predicate never evaluates - a clinician reads a mcg/kg/min dose, an FC > 130 bpm criterion, or a criterion number the trigger did not verify; the displayed rationale and the firing condition diverge. Affected: RULE-ALERTAS-003, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-007, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-009, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-011, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-012, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-015, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-024, RULE-EFICIENCIA-002, RULE-EFICIENCIA-003, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-002, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-003, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-011, RULE-PROFILAXIA-008, RULE-SEDACAO-020, RULE-SEPSE-043, RULE-SEPSE-058, RULE-SEPSE-059.

facts.title: v1-vs-v3 AND/OR aggregation divergence (absence-filter AND-collapse)
facts.claim: Criteria the reference (and the docstring) define as OR alternatives are implemented as a single Django .filter() with comma-separated (AND) conditions, or vice-versa. Most dangerous instance: 'no sedation / no vasopressor' confounder-exclusion clauses - an AND across all sedatives requires every drug present simultaneously (practically never true), so the exclusion never fires and a fully sedated patient can be flagged. Mirror defect drops OR-alternative positive criteria so an alert under-fires. Affected: RULE-EFICIENCIA-005, RULE-EFICIENCIA-008, RULE-EFICIENCIA-010, RULE-SEDACAO-004, RULE-SEDACAO-008, RULE-SEPSE-014, RULE-SEPSE-021, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-010.

facts.title: Chained-comparison Python misparse suppressing the severe band
facts.claim: Expressions of the form 'if 7 <= dor > 10:' are parsed by Python as (7 <= dor) and (dor > 10). Because the input validator caps the scale at its maximum (10, 12, ...), the second clause is impossible, so the most severe band never fires - the highest-acuity pain/agitation grade is silently suppressed. Intended code was 'low <= x <= high'. Affected: RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-006, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-007, RULE-SEDACAO-018.

facts.title: Off-by-one / exclusive-range boundary (dead gaps at band edges)
facts.claim: Two flavors: (a) Python range(a, b) stop bound is exclusive, so boundary values (e.g. status 399, creatinine 5.0) fall through to the wrong branch; (b) strict </> used where the reference uses <=/>=, leaving uncovered gaps at band edges that resolve to None/0 - e.g. SOFA creatinine (4.9, 5.0], bilirubin [1.9, 2.0), [5.9, 6.0), [11.9, 12.0). These undercount at the top of the scale, exactly where acuity is highest. Affected: RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-002, RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-004, RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-007, RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-009, RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-010, RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-013, RULE-EFICIENCIA-003, RULE-EFICIENCIA-010, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-005, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-019, RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-005, RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-032, RULE-SEDACAO-010, RULE-SEPSE-040, RULE-SEPSE-058, RULE-VENTILACAO-012.

facts.title: Dead / unwired / unreachable criterion code
facts.claim: Whole criteria are computed but never wired into the aggregation (calcular_criterio_N commented out), or gated behind a predicate that is structurally always-False (e.g. a diagnosis filter built with vars(...).fromkeys(...) that keys on field NAMES), or written so a band is unreachable. These are silent false-negatives: the criterion appears to exist but can never contribute to an alert. Affected: RULE-EFICIENCIA-005, RULE-SEPSE-014, RULE-SEPSE-006, RULE-SEPSE-009, RULE-SEPSE-019, RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-007, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-006, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-007, RULE-PIORA-CLINICA-010, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-001, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-005, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-008, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-009, RULE-ESTABILIDADE-011, RULE-SEDACAO-003, RULE-SEDACAO-004, RULE-SEDACAO-008, RULE-SEDACAO-010, RULE-SEDACAO-011, RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-009, RULE-NUTRICAO-004, RULE-MOVIMENTACAO-ADT-036.

facts.title: Weight decimal-separator parse (~10x inflation)
facts.claim: Weight is parsed with float(''.join(re.findall(r'\d+', peso.replace(',', '.')))), which strips the decimal point entirely: '70,5' / '70.5' -> 705 kg. Every weight-normalized threshold (mL/kg/h urine output, mcg/kg dosing) is then ~10x off, and any bed/occupancy or capacity count built on the same parsing style is likewise corrupted. Affected: RULE-CLINICAL-SCORING-002, RULE-SEPSE-014, RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-003, RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-004, RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-012.

facts.title: Month-agnostic day-of-month date filtering
facts.claim: A shared window-construction helper filters on criado_em__day, which matches the day number regardless of month/year, so readings from the same day-number in a different month contaminate the 07:00-07:00 balance/vitals windows (over- or under-counting fluid balance, urine output, and peak temperature). A related boundary defect uses timezone.now() without .astimezone(), computing month/year boundaries in the wrong timezone and silently excluding month-end rows despite an 'inclusive' intent. Affected: RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-006, RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-007, RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-008, RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-009, RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-010, RULE-BALANCO-HIDRICO-011, RULE-AUDITORIA-LOGS-001, RULE-OPERACIONAL-INFRA-010, RULE-TENANCY-ORGANIZACAO-015.

facts.title: SOFA respiratory sub-score (PaO2/FiO2)
facts.claim: Standard P/F cutoffs (400/300/200/100) require FiO2 as a fraction (0.21-1.0), but DadosProntuario.FiO2Validator stores FiO2 as 21-100 (percentage) - with percentage FiO2 the ratio is ~100x too small, so nearly every ventilated patient scores 4 (worst) instead of the true band. Reference also gates the 3- and 4-point bands on mechanical ventilation status; legacy applies them unconditionally, and tests set fio2=1 directly, bypassing the validator.

facts.title: SOFA cardiovascular sub-score (vasopressors/MAP)
facts.claim: Standard SOFA cardiovascular uses vasopressor RATES in mcg/kg/min (norepinephrine >0.1=4, <=0.1=3; dopamine/epinephrine tiers; dobutamine any=2; MAP<70=1). Legacy reads noradrenaline as a raw ml VOLUME (Noradrenalina.quantidade) with the 3/4-point split at >10 ml, and dobutamine any>0=2; MAP<70=1 matches, but dopamine and epinephrine are omitted entirely.

facts.title: SOFA renal sub-score (creatinine/urine output)
facts.claim: Standard SOFA renal (Cr mg/dL): <1.2=0, 1.2-1.9=1, 2.0-3.4=2, 3.5-4.9=3, >=5.0=4; urine <500=3, <200=4. Legacy's '2 points' band is written 2.0-4.0 (standard 2.0-3.4) and the 4-point test is strict '> 5', leaving a dead gap at (4.9, 5.0] - creatinine exactly 5.0 mg/dL scores 0 instead of 4, a 4-point undercount at the top of the scale.

facts.title: Piora Clinica criterio_6 - Dor (escala numerica 0-10)
facts.claim: 'if 7 <= dor > 10' parses as (7 <= dor) AND (dor > 10); since escala_dor (0-10 NRS) is capped at 10, dor > 10 is impossible, so severe pain (NRS 7-10) always scores 0 and never triggers the grade-3 (VERMELHO) band. Intended code was '7 <= dor <= 10 -> 3+'; secondary: legacy's 1+ band starts at dor=2, not 1 as in reference.

facts.title: Piora Clinica criterio_7 - Dor (escala comportamental 3-12)
facts.claim: Identical bug pattern to criterio_6: 'if 10 <= sinais > 12' parses as (10 <= sinais) AND (sinais > 12); sinais_dor (BPS, range 3-12) is capped at 12, so sinais > 12 is impossible and the severe band (BPS 10-12) always scores 0. Intended '10 <= sinais <= 12 -> 3+'; affects the non-communicative/sedated population BPS exists for.

facts.title: Estabilidade facade alert-text - vasopressor/inotrope escalation ladder (criteria 7-9)
facts.claim: Facade dosing text is in noradrenaline mcg/kg/min (>0.5, >1.5) while the paired v3 predicates trigger on qt_vol_nora infusion volume in ml/h (RULE-007 >20 ml/h; RULE-008 >70 ml/h + vaso>5 ml/h) - not convertible without drug concentration/patient weight. criterio_9 text centres on FC > 130 bpm tachycardia, but v3 criterio_9 tests only noradrenaline>50 ml/h AND dobutamine>10 ml/h and never checks FC.

facts.title: PaO2/FiO2 ratio (relacao PO2/FiO2)
facts.claim: FiO2Validator restricts fio2 to 0 or 21-100 (percentage), but every clinical threshold using this ratio (SOFA 400/300/200/100; ventilacao 150/200/250/300) and all tests (fio2=1 -> ratio=po2) treat fio2 as a fraction 0.21-1.0; with percentage FiO2 the ratio is ~100x too small. The same ratio is also used with fio2 as a fraction (<0.4) in ventilacao criterio_8, and divided by 100 in the FiO2xPEEP table - internally inconsistent on FiO2 units.

facts.title: SEPSE v3 criterio_8 - oliguria without vasopressor/dialysis
facts.claim: No noradrenaline(12h) AND no hemodialysis AND 'DRC em TRS' in diagnosis AND urine output <0.5 mL/kg/h over 12h. The DRC gate is built via vars(evolucao).fromkeys(...), which keys on field NAMES (never == 'DRC em TRS'), always yielding [] -> False, forcing all([]) so the criterion NEVER fires; separately the weight parse strips the decimal separator ('70,5'/'70.5' -> 705 kg), inflating weight ~10x.

facts.title: Eficiencia v3 criterio_9 - coma without sedation (defined, unwired)
facts.claim: Intended: GCS<6 AND absence of any sedative (OR across cetamina/dexmedetomidina/propofol/tiopental/midazolam/fenitoina/fenobarbital) in the last 6h -> suspected brain death. Code implements GCS<13 (not <6) and an AND-combined sedative filter requiring ALL sedatives present simultaneously (practically never true), defeating the confounder exclusion; the criterion is also unwired (calcular_criterio_9 commented out).

facts.title: Piora Clinica - Calculo do alerta (soma agregada + gatilho por criterio)
facts.claim: Aggregates 9 graded sub-scores into a color alert (0-7 NEUTRO, 8-14 AMARELO, 15-21 VERMELHO sum bands, else per-criterion grade-2/3 override). payload['alerta']/['mensagem'] are reassigned every loop iteration so the HIGHEST-INDEX grade-2/3 criterion wins, letting a later AMARELO overwrite an earlier VERMELHO - downgrading a red alert to yellow; soma += int(criterio[0]) reads only the first char, discarding +/- direction.

facts.title: Ventilation C8 - extubation-readiness bundle
facts.claim: Extubation-readiness bundle (awake, >1d VM, low PEEP/FiO2, adequate FR/oxygenation, no noradrenaline) treats FiO2 as a FRACTION (fio2 < 0.4), while sibling criteria C2/C3 (RULE-VENTILACAO-004/005) divide fio2 by 100 as a PERCENTAGE, and the movimentacao data-entry form validates FiO2 as 21-100 (percent). If production stores FiO2 as a percentage, 40<0.4 is always False and this alert-forcing readiness criterion never triggers.

facts.title: Sepsis C6 (major) - hypotension (PAS<90 or PAD<90 in 24h)
facts.claim: Major criterion: current PAS < 90 mmHg OR most-recent-24h diastolic PAD < 90 mmHg. _REGRAS text says 'PAS < 90 OU PAD < 60'; code compares PAD < 90 mmHg, not 60 mmHg. Because normal diastolic BP is ~60-80 mmHg, PAD < 90 is satisfied by nearly every patient with a recorded diastolic value, so this MAJOR criterion is nearly always True.

constraints.text: Nothing in ESCALATIONS.md has been corrected - every rule is captured exactly as the legacy system behaves (unit mismatches, dead branches, off-by-one boundaries, inverted predicates all preserved verbatim). Whether legacy behavior is intended (carry forward) or a defect (change) is a clinician/product-owner decision, explicitly out of scope for the audit.

All 348 escalated items are captured in ESCALATIONS.md, each in exactly one primary section, preserved verbatim from the legacy snapshots; no item in this report was corrected in the reconciled catalog.

Each of the 12 P0 items (P0-01..P0-12) is a verified divergence (extraction AND verification both agree) that can directly mis-score, mis-dose, or mis-triage a patient. Per the platform rule-disposition policy, every P0-band rule MUST receive disposition RATIFY - never silently ADOPT/ADOPT-CORRECTED/ADAPT/SUPERSEDE/RETIRE - pending a human clinical decision.
constraints.source_ref: ESCALATIONS.md §P0 header, lines 40-42; CONTRACTS.md rule-disposition policy

constraints.text: ml/h vasopressor infusion rate and mcg/kg/min weight-based rate are NOT interconvertible without drug concentration and patient weight - any downstream design MUST NOT assume a fixed conversion factor between them, nor treat facade-displayed doses as verified against the firing predicate.
constraints.source_ref: ESCALATIONS.md §P0 RULE-ESTABILIDADE-016, line 121-123; §Cross-cutting systemic issue #2, line 1099

open_questions: All 22 items above (10 systemic patterns + 12 P0 escalations) require RATIFY disposition per CONTRACTS.md - none may be silently resolved by a downstream designer; canonical unit decisions (FiO2 fraction-vs-percent, lactate mg/dL-vs-mmol/L, vasopressor mcg/kg/min-vs-ml/h) must be made once, centrally, before any rule referencing them is designed.
